title: Slovakian Pork Stew
description: A hearty German-inspired stew made with tender pork and Slovakian flair.
instructions: In a large Dutch oven, brown the pork cubes over medium-high heat.
Remove the pork from the pot and set aside.
Add the sliced onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the sliced carrots and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Add the cubed potatoes, beef broth, tomato paste, and dried thyme to the pot.
Return the browned pork to the pot and bring to a boil.
Cover the pot with a lid and transfer to the preheated oven.
Bake for 2 1/2 hours, or until the pork is tender and the potatoes are cooked through.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
